params.name: Suger's Eagle
params.description:
On Nov 9, 1147 AD, the vase was created by a craftsmen commissioned by Suger of the Saint Denis Abbey.
Suger's Eagle is an eagle-shaped bottle commissioned by Suger of the Saint Denis Abbey and created by skilled craftsmen. Its body is an ancient porphyry bottle, while its head, wings, and claws are gold-plated and embossed with silver. It was used as a mass item in the Saint-Denis Abbey during the priest's commemoration of Jesus' communion. The exaggerated neck and wings of the eagle mark th …
